news.title: In vivo CAR T cell generation using retargeted and functionalized lentiviral vectors with reduced immunogenicity
news.summary: CAR T cell therapies are effective but costly and difficult to manufacture. Here, authors develop a novel approach using retargeted and functionalized lentiviral vectors that selectively reprogram T cells in vivo, evade immune clearance, and generate function…
news.sowhat: In vivo CAR-T generation via engineered lentiviruses could bypass manufacturing bottlenecks and reduce therapy costs significantly.

news.title: CancerVax Reports Successful Initial Mouse Study with Breakthrough Results
news.summary: First biodistribution study of the Company’s novel targeted lipid nanoparticle achieved systemic circulation with the potential to target many types of cancer First biodistribution study of the Company’s novel targeted lipid nanoparticle achieved systemic cir…
news.sowhat: CancerVax's targeted LNP platform demonstrates systemic delivery capability, potentially expanding personalized cancer immunotherapy reach.

news.title: $4.26 Billion Brain Tumor Drugs Market 2026: Trends and Growth Outlook to 2030 & 2035
news.summary: Key market opportunities include the growth of precision medicine, AI integration in drug discovery, increased R&D in oncology, rising adoption of targeted therapies, and technological innovations like targeted RAF kinase inhibitors. North America leads, with…

news.title: Biocytogen Grants Taisho Pharmaceutical a License to its RenNano® Fully Human Heavy Chain-Only Antibody Discovery Platform
news.summary: BEIJING & TOKYO — Biocytogen Pharmaceuticals (Beijing) Co., Ltd. (Biocytogen, SSE: 688796; HKEX: 02315), a global biotechnology company that drives the research and development of novel antibody-based drugs with innovative technologies, today announced that i…
news.sowhat: Biocytogen's heavy chain-only antibody platform enables Taisho to develop potentially superior CAR-T targeting agents with enhanced tissue penetration.

news.title: Engineering in vivo CAR-T cells
news.summary: Early clinical data on in vivo CAR-T cell therapy for multiple myeloma reveals key mechanistic insights and could mark the beginning of a new era of fast, streamlined and accessible immunotherapies.
news.sowhat: In vivo CAR-T enables faster, simpler manufacturing while maintaining efficacy in multiple myeloma—potentially democratizing cell therapy.

news.title: In vivo generation of anti-BCMA CAR-T cells in relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma: a phase 1 study
news.summary: In a phase 1 trial, the in vivo generation of anti-BCMA CAR-T cells by lentiviral delivery was feasible and did not lead to dose-limiting toxicities in five patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.
news.sowhat: In vivo CAR-T generation via lentiviral delivery shows feasibility without dose-limiting toxicity in MM patients.

news.title: Tessera Therapeutics Receives Grant to Advance In Vivo Gene Writing Approaches Towards a Potential Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) Cure
news.summary: Funding from the Gates Foundation supports development of multiple in vivo Gene Writing strategies aimed at enabling a scalable cure for HIV Funding from the Gates Foundation supports development of multiple in vivo Gene Writing strategies aimed at enabling a…
news.sowhat: Tessera's Gates-funded gene writing platform could enable scalable HIV cures without ex vivo cell manufacturing.

news.title: Azalea Therapeutics Highlights Nature Publication Demonstrating In Vivo Site-Specific TRAC-CAR T Cell Engineering
news.summary: Azalea highlights Nature publication demonstrating in vivo CAR T engineering through site-specific genomic integration with durable anti-tumor activity....
news.sowhat: Azalea's in vivo TRAC-CAR engineering achieves durable tumor control via direct genomic integration, bypassing ex vivo manufacturing.

news.title: CRISPR makes enhanced cancer-fighting immune cells inside mice
news.summary: Gene-editing technique promises a potentially safer way to create CAR T cells with a simple injection.
news.sowhat: In vivo CRISPR gene-editing could eliminate manufacturing complexity, reducing CAR-T production costs and timelines significantly.

news.summary: Key opportunities in the B-cell inhibitors market for non-oncology diseases include the rising demand for targeted immunomodulatory therapies, driven by increasing autoimmune disorder prevalence. Strategic partnerships and innovation efforts focus on overcomi…
news.sowhat: B-cell inhibitor expansion in autoimmune space signals adjacent market opportunity for CAR-T developers.

news.title: Next-generation programmable cell therapies for precision medicine
news.summary: In this Review, Teixeira et al. discuss emerging strategies for developing and improving engineered-cell therapies. They outline progress from ex vivo engineered autologous cells to in vivo reprogramming, advances in delivery systems and the remaining transla…
news.sowhat: In vivo reprogramming shifts CAR-T manufacturing from ex vivo engineering to direct patient body cell conversion.

news.title: B cell depletion in autoimmune disease: in vivo CAR-T or T cell engagers?
news.summary: Deep B cell depletion is delivering transformative clinical results in autoimmune diseases. Dealmakers are wagering on the best tools for the job.
news.sowhat: In vivo CAR-T competes with T cell engagers as the preferred platform for B cell depletion in autoimmune disease.
